---
title: "sphere_grid_pad_2d"
generated: true
description: "Pad a 2D parameterised-sphere image with the sphere topology."
---

[â€ą back to `nitrix.geometry.sphere_grid`](../nitrix-geometry-sphere_grid.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:sphere_grid_pad_2d -->
```python
sphere_grid_pad_2d(image: Num[Array, '... H W ...'], pad: PadSpec, *, height_axis: int = -2, width_axis: int = -1, pole_negate_channel: Optional[int] = None, pole_negate_axis: int = -1) -> Num[Array, '... H_padded W_padded ...']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:sphere_grid_pad_2d -->
Pad a 2D parameterised-sphere image with the sphere topology.

The padding scheme:

- **Longitudinal** axis (`width_axis`) is treated as a
  circular wrap: the first `w_pad` columns are taken from
  the rightmost `w_pad` of the input, and vice versa.
- **Latitudinal** axis (`height_axis`) is pole-bounded:
  the top `h_pad` rows are built from the rows just inside
  the north pole (rows `1 : h_pad + 1`), *flipped vertically*
  and *rolled horizontally by* `W // 2`.  The bottom
  `h_pad` rows are symmetric: rows `H - h_pad - 1 : H - 1`,
  flipped and rolled.  The pole rows themselves (`row 0` and
  `row H - 1`) are NOT used for reflection -- a pole row is
  a (compressed) point on the sphere, and reflecting it would
  duplicate it.

For 2D flow / vector fields, the longitudinal component of the
flow reverses sign across each pole (because longitude reverses
on pole crossing).  Pass `pole_negate_channel` to identify
which channel-axis entry to negate in the pole-padded rows.
The latitudinal flow component does NOT reverse and should be
left alone.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:sphere_grid_pad_2d -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `image` | `Num` | `...` Ă— `H` Ă— `W` Ă— `...` | â€” | Channel-last image, `(..., H, W, ...)`. `H` and `W` are at `height_axis` and `width_axis`; everything else is preserved. |
| `pad` | `PadSpec` | â€” | â€” | Pad amount. `int` -> symmetric `h_pad = w_pad = pad`; `(h_pad, w_pad)` -> per-axis. After padding the height becomes `H + 2 * h_pad` and the width `W + 2 * w_pad`. |
| `height_axis` | `int` | â€” | `-2` | Axis index for the latitudinal axis. Default `-2` assumes a trailing channel axis (or scalar 2D image with no trailing channel; either works -- the axis selection is explicit). |
| `width_axis` | `int` | â€” | `-1` | Axis index for the longitudinal axis. Default `-1` (last axis). Note: if you pass channel-last images `(*, H, W, C)`, set `height_axis=-3`, `width_axis=-2`. |
| `pole_negate_channel` | `Optional[int]` | â€” | `None` | If not `None`, index along `pole_negate_axis` whose entries should be sign-flipped in the pole-padded rows. Used for the longitudinal-flow component of 2D flow fields. |
| `pole_negate_axis` | `int` | â€” | `-1` | Axis along which `pole_negate_channel` indexes. Default `-1` (last axis). Only consulted when `pole_negate_channel` is not `None`. |

**Returns** `Num` â€” shape `...` Ă— `H_padded` Ă— `W_padded` Ă— `...`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen perf:sphere_grid_pad_2d -->
:::: {.callout-note title="Benchmarks â€” nitrix-perf-bench" collapse="true"}
GPU steady-state **94.0 Âµs** (median, L4); up to **2.45Ă— faster** than `cupy.sphere_grid_pad_2d`.  [full report](https://github.com/hypercoil/nitrix-perf-bench/blob/main/reports/PERF_SPHERE_GRID_PAD_2D.md)

::: {.table-responsive}
| case | platform | param | baseline | status | steady (min/med) | compile | mem | fidelity | ratio |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| sphere_grid_pad_2d | jax-cpu | h=128 | `cupy.sphere_grid_pad_2d` | skipped | â€” | â€” | â€” | â€” | â€” |
| sphere_grid_pad_2d | jax-cpu | h=128 | `nitrix-jax` | ok | 31.7 Âµs / 34.2 Âµs | 142.05 ms | 701 MB (rss) | âś“ 0Ă—tol | 1.00x vs nitrix-jax |
| sphere_grid_pad_2d | jax-cpu | h=128 | `numpy.sphere_grid_pad` | ok | 30.2 Âµs / 32.1 Âµs | 59.9 Âµs | 701 MB (rss) | âś“ 0Ă—tol | 0.95x vs nitrix-jax |
| sphere_grid_pad_2d | jax-cuda12 | h=128 | `cupy.sphere_grid_pad_2d` | ok | 218.6 Âµs / 228.0 Âµs | 42.24 ms | 0.13 MB (hbm) | âś“ 0Ă—tol | 2.40x vs nitrix-jax |
| sphere_grid_pad_2d | jax-cuda12 | h=128 | `nitrix-jax` | ok | 91.0 Âµs / 94.0 Âµs | 130.11 ms | 0.42 MB (hbm) | âś“ 0Ă—tol | 1.00x vs nitrix-jax |
| sphere_grid_pad_2d | jax-cuda12 | h=128 | `numpy.sphere_grid_pad` | ok | 28.9 Âµs / 29.5 Âµs | 51.5 Âµs | 0.13 MB (hbm) | âś“ 0Ă—tol | 0.32x vs nitrix-jax |
| sphere_grid_pad_2d | jax-cpu | h=256 | `cupy.sphere_grid_pad_2d` | skipped | â€” | â€” | â€” | â€” | â€” |
| sphere_grid_pad_2d | jax-cpu | h=256 | `nitrix-jax` | ok | 104.3 Âµs / 129.2 Âµs | 124.46 ms | 701 MB (rss) | âś“ 0Ă—tol | 1.00x vs nitrix-jax |
| sphere_grid_pad_2d | jax-cpu | h=256 | `numpy.sphere_grid_pad` | ok | 62.4 Âµs / 68.8 Âµs | 92.3 Âµs | 701 MB (rss) | âś“ 0Ă—tol | 0.60x vs nitrix-jax |
| sphere_grid_pad_2d | jax-cuda12 | h=256 | `cupy.sphere_grid_pad_2d` | ok | 224.0 Âµs / 231.2 Âµs | 59.49 ms | 0.52 MB (hbm) | âś“ 0Ă—tol | 2.45x vs nitrix-jax |
| sphere_grid_pad_2d | jax-cuda12 | h=256 | `nitrix-jax` | ok | 91.6 Âµs / 97.0 Âµs | 154.09 ms | 2.10 MB (hbm) | âś“ 0Ă—tol | 1.00x vs nitrix-jax |
| sphere_grid_pad_2d | jax-cuda12 | h=256 | `numpy.sphere_grid_pad` | ok | 60.9 Âµs / 62.0 Âµs | 82.7 Âµs | 0.52 MB (hbm) | âś“ 0Ă—tol | 0.66x vs nitrix-jax |
| sphere_grid_pad_2d | jax-cpu | h=64 | `cupy.sphere_grid_pad_2d` | skipped | â€” | â€” | â€” | â€” | â€” |
| sphere_grid_pad_2d | jax-cpu | h=64 | `nitrix-jax` | ok | 14.9 Âµs / 15.2 Âµs | 136.22 ms | 701 MB (rss) | âś“ 0Ă—tol | 1.00x vs nitrix-jax |
| sphere_grid_pad_2d | jax-cpu | h=64 | `numpy.sphere_grid_pad` | ok | 34.6 Âµs / 36.8 Âµs | 67.2 Âµs | 701 MB (rss) | âś“ 0Ă—tol | 2.32x vs nitrix-jax |
| sphere_grid_pad_2d | jax-cuda12 | h=64 | `cupy.sphere_grid_pad_2d` | ok | 219.9 Âµs / 234.9 Âµs | 73.44 ms | 0.03 MB (hbm) | âś“ 0Ă—tol | 1.93x vs nitrix-jax |
| sphere_grid_pad_2d | jax-cuda12 | h=64 | `nitrix-jax` | ok | 113.8 Âµs / 119.8 Âµs | 140.91 ms | 0.11 MB (hbm) | âś“ 0Ă—tol | 1.00x vs nitrix-jax |
| sphere_grid_pad_2d | jax-cuda12 | h=64 | `numpy.sphere_grid_pad` | ok | 20.7 Âµs / 21.7 Âµs | 40.7 Âµs | 0.03 MB (hbm) | âś“ 0Ă—tol | 0.18x vs nitrix-jax |
:::

*source: bench `da5314ba` Â· nitrix `f42e7ff8`*
::::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:sphere_grid_pad_2d -->
::: {.callout-note}
This is the right padding scheme for 2D conv / morphology /
smoothing on equirectangular-parameterised spherical images
(FreeSurfer surface registration, surface-based saliency, etc.)
The pole flip + W/2 roll is the natural "go over the pole"
topology of an equirectangular grid.

For a spherical-diffeomorphism warp this composes directly with
the existing sampler -- no sphere-aware sampler is needed: pad the
stationary velocity field here (with `pole_negate_channel` for
the longitudinal flow component), integrate it with
[`integrate_velocity_field`](../nitrix-geometry-grid/integrate_velocity_field.qmd), then
resample with
[`spatial_transform`](../nitrix-geometry-grid/spatial_transform.qmd) using
`mode='nearest'`.  The longitudinal seam is handled by this
padding (equivalent to `mode='wrap'`) and the over-the-pole halo
by the flip + roll; `mode='nearest'` then edge-replicates the
(already topology-correct) padded borders.
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:sphere_grid_pad_2d -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `H` | *(undocumented â€” add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `W` | *(undocumented â€” add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `H_padded` | *(undocumented â€” add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `W_padded` | *(undocumented â€” add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
